title: Permission denied when running "podman machine ssh" on macOS
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug  


**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

When trying to run podman for the first time on Mac, I get denied permission to ssh into the VM. 

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Run podman machine init

2. Run podman machine start

3. Run podman machine ssh

**Describe the results you received:**
Got this error: 

```
me@MacBook ~ % podman machine ssh        
Connecting to vm podman-machine-default. To close connection, use `~.` or `exit`
Warning: Permanently added '[localhost]:49886' (ED25519) to the list of known hosts.
johndoe@localhost: Permission denied (publickey,gssapi-keyex,gssapi-with-mic).
Error: exit status 255
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
To show a shell for the default user in the vm.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

I think Podman is making a mistake here. I had a user called johndoe on a remote system at 10.10.10.10:12345(ssh). It appears Podman is mistaking it with the user that it's creating for the VM, hence the permission error. 
**Output of `podman version`:**

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


```
Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman. failed to create sshClient: Connection to bastion host (ssh://johndoe@10.10.10.10:12345/run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock) failed.: ssh: handshake failed: read tcp 10.18.0.3:50267->10.10.10.10:12345: read: connection reset by peer 
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `brew info podman`):**

```
podman: stable 3.4.4 (bottled), HEAD
Tool for managing OCI containers and pods
https://podman.io/
/usr/local/Cellar/podman/3.4.4 (170 files, 40MB) *
  Poured from bottle on 2022-01-14 at 09:31:35
From: https://github.com/Homebrew/homebrew-core/blob/HEAD/Formula/podman.rb
License: Apache-2.0
==> Dependencies
Build: go ✘, go-md2man ✘
Required: qemu ✔
==> Options
--HEAD
	Install HEAD version
==> Caveats
zsh completions have been installed to:
  /usr/local/share/zsh/site-functions
==> Analytics
install: 16,336 (30 days), 40,310 (90 days), 96,874 (365 days)
install-on-request: 16,333 (30 days), 40,310 (90 days), 96,863 (365 days)
build-error: 4 (30 days)
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):** 

**OS**  
macOS Monterey (12.1)

comments.body: any chance you have more than a couple of ssh keys?

Yes, I suppose I do. I have several ssh keys for connecting to different git repositories online for example, and they start with id_rsa_*. Can this cause problems?

    On Sunday, February 13, 2022, 10:46:31 AM PST, Brent Baude ***@***.***> wrote:  
 
 


any chance you have more than a couple of ssh keys?

—
Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub, or unsubscribe.
You are receiving this because you authored the thread.Message ID: ***@***.***>

I was able to log into the VM by running:  
```
podman machine ssh podman-machine-default
```
podman-machine-default was the only vm I saw in the output for `podman machine list`.  I don't know what was happening previously.

Just a question I'm wondering is, "how is port forwarding done inside the VM to macOS?" I imagine when inside the VM, host is the VM itself not macOS. So, how come using "--publish 8000:80" actually forwards port 80 from the container running inside the VM to the macOS operating system?
Is this fixed now?  Can we close this issue?
Yes, my problem is fixed. I left the issue open in case you want to add something on why this happens or how this can be prevented.
Closing, looks like this has been resolved.
